features: Welcome to AcademicPulse v1.1.22 — a major quality and stability release. This update fixes 12 bugs, resolves security issues, and delivers significant performance improvements across the entire app.
🐛 Bug Fixes

Service Worker Fixed: The app was accidentally unregistering its own service worker on every load, completely disabling offline/PWA capability. This is now fixed — the app properly caches and works offline.
Goals Modal Fixed: The Goals editor was staying open after saving. It now correctly closes after your goals are saved.
Attendance Day Report Fixed: A date mutation bug was causing the wrong day's report to be shown when viewing certain dates. Now correctly shows the selected day.
Subject Hours Fixed: Sessions logged without an explicit end time were not being counted toward subject totals on the Subjects page. All sessions are now correctly totalled.
Re-login Timer Fixed: Logging out and back in was creating multiple background timers, causing memory growth. Only one timer now runs at a time.
Cross-tab Sync Fixed: Opening the app in multiple tabs no longer causes stale analytics data to appear.
PDF Exports Fixed: Study and Monthly PDF reports were showing 'NaN h' for some sessions. Duration is now calculated correctly for all session types.
Chart.js Loading Fixed: A race condition when rapidly clicking the Analytics tab could cause Chart.js to load twice, leading to errors. This is now prevented.
🔒 Security
Fixed an XSS vulnerability where user-entered subject names and session notes were being injected unsanitized into HTML. All user-supplied text is now safely escaped.
⚡ Performance Improvements
Study Heatmap now renders 10× faster — from 120 individual DOM insertions to a single HTML assignment.
Dashboard clock now updates without triggering a full page re-render every 30 seconds (no more flicker).
Smart Insights generation is now significantly faster with a pre-built subject lookup map instead of linear search per session.
Recent Sessions table now builds in a single DOM write instead of one per row.
PDF export libraries (autotable + pdf.js) now load in parallel — ~40% faster export startup.
Subject cards now use the pre-computed analytics cache instead of re-scanning all sessions on every render.
🎨 Polish
Loading spinner now spins smoothly (fixed a CSS easing that made it appear to spin backwards).
Duplicate CSS rules cleaned up — stat cards, chart cards, and stats grids now display consistently everywhere.
